name: yellowdog-python-examples

metadata.name: yellowdog-python-examples
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Example Python commands using the YellowDog Python SDK

documentation:
# Example Python Commands using the YellowDog Python SDK

## Overview

This is a set of command line Python scripts for interacting with the YellowDog Platform, providing examples of usage of the [YellowDog Python SDK](https://docs.yellowdog.co/api/python/index.html).

The command scripts support:

- **Provisioning** Worker Pools with the **`yd-provision`** command
- **Submitting** Work Requirements with the **`yd-submit`** command
- **Starting** HELD Work Requirements and **Holding** (or pausing) RUNNING Work Requirements with the **`yd-start`** and **`yd-hold`** commands
- **Uploading** files to the YellowDog Object Store with the **`yd-upload`** command
- **Instantiating** Compute Requirements with the **`yd-instantiate`** command
- **Downloading** Results from the YellowDog Object Store with the **`yd-download`** command
- **Aborting** running Tasks with the **`yd-abort`** command
- **Cancelling** Work Requirements with the **`yd-cancel`** command
- **Shutting Down** Worker Pools with the **`yd-shutdown`** command
- **Terminating** Compute Requirements with the **`yd-terminate`** command
- **Deleting** objects in the YellowDog Object Store with the **`yd-delete`** command
- **Listing** YellowDog items using the **`yd-list`** command
- **Resizing** Worker Pools and Compute Requirements with the **`yd-resize`** command
- **Boosting** Allowances with the **`yd-boost`** command
- **Creating, Updating and Removing** Source Templates, Compute Templates, Keyrings, Credentials, Namespace Storage Configurations, Image Families, Allowances, and Configured Worker Pools with the **`yd-create`** and **`yd-remove`** commands
- **Following Event Streams** for Work Requirements, Worker Pools and Compute Requirements with the **`yd-follow`** command
- **Showing** the details of any YellowDog entity by its YellowDog ID with the **`yd-show`** command

Please see the documenation in the [GitHub repository](https://github.com/yellowdog/python-examples) for more details.

functions.name: yd_commands.entity_utils.get_compute_requirement_id_by_worker_pool_id
documentation: Get a Compute Requirement ID from a Provisioned Worker Pool ID.

functions.name: yd_commands.entity_utils.get_all_worker_pools
documentation: Return all Worker Pool summaries.

functions.name: yd_commands.entity_utils.find_image_family_reference_by_name
documentation:
Resolve image family references. Complicated logic.
Fully qualified name is used for non-ambiguous PRIVATE image families.

functions.name: yd_commands.entity_utils.clear_image_family_search_cache
documentation: Clear the cache of Image Family name searches.

functions.name: yd_commands.entity_utils.remove_allowances_matching_description

Set the Work Requirement to be represented, processed to

Represent a complete Work Requirement, with Tasks included within

modules.name: yd_commands.utils
documentation: General utility functions.

functions.name: yd_commands.utils.unpack_namespace_in_prefix
documentation:
Allow the prefix to include the namespace, which can override the supplied
namespace. Return the unpacked (namespace, prefix) tuple.

functions.name: yd_commands.utils.pathname_relative_to_config_file
documentation:
Find the pathname of a file relative to the location
of the config file

functions.name: yd_commands.utils.generate_id
documentation: Add a UTC timestamp and check length.

functions.name: yd_commands.utils.link_entity
documentation: Utility functions for creating links to YD entities

functions.name: yd_commands.utils.add_batch_number_postfix
documentation: Generate a name using batch details.

functions.name: yd_commands.utils.get_delimited_string_boundaries
documentation:
Given an input string and a pair of opening and closing delimiter strings,
find the list of start and end indices for the top-level strings enclosed
by the opening and closing delimiters.

For example:
    if input_string = "abc {{{{x}}_hello}} 234 {{{{world}}}}",
    and opening_delimiter = "{{", closing_delimiter = "}}",
    the function will return Substring objects: [(4, 19), (24, 37)]

Opening and closing delimiters must be balanced across the entire
input_string, otherwise an exception will be raised.

functions.name: yd_commands.utils.split_delimited_string
documentation:
This function takes a string containing delimited sections and breaks it
into a list of strings, including the non-delimited sections.
Delimiters are retained.

For example:
  when called with ("one{{two}}three{{{{four}}}}five", "{{", "}}")
  the result is: ['one', '{{two}}', 'three', '{{{{four}}}}', 'five']

functions.name: yd_commands.utils.remove_outer_delimiters
documentation:
Remove the outermost delimiters from a string.
There is no checking for a well-formed string.

functions.name: yd_commands.utils.format_yd_name
documentation: Format a string to be consistent with YellowDog naming requirements.

classes.name: yd_commands.utils.Substring
documentation: Functions for handling delimited variables within strings.

modules.name: yd_commands.provision_utils
documentation: Utility functions for provisioning and instantiating.

functions.name: yd_commands.provision_utils.get_user_data_property
documentation:
Get the 'userData' property, either using the string specified in
'userData', the file specified in 'userDataFile', or a concatenation
of the files listed in 'userDataFiles'.
Raise exception if more than one of these properties is set.

functions.name: yd_commands.provision_utils.get_template_id
documentation:
Check if 'template_id_or_name' looks like a valid CRT ID; if not,
assume it's a CRT name and perform a lookup.

functions.name: yd_commands.provision_utils.get_image_family_id
documentation:
Check if 'image_id_or_name' looks like a valid IF ID; if not,
assume it's an IF name and perform a lookup.

functions.name: yd_commands.create._get_model_class

functions.name: yd_commands.create.create_attribute_definition

functions.name: yd_commands.create.create_namespace_policy

modules.name: yd_commands.submit
documentation: A script to submit a Work Requirement.
!/usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: yd_commands.submit.submit_work_requirement
documentation:
Submit a Work Requirement defined in a tasks_data dictionary.
Supply either tasks_data or task_count.

The general principle with configuration properties is that a property set
at a lower level will override its setting at higher levels, so:

Task > Task Group > Top-Level JSON Property > TOML config file

functions.name: yd_commands.submit.create_task_group
documentation: Create a TaskGroup object.

functions.name: yd_commands.submit.add_tasks_to_task_group
documentation: Add all the constituent Tasks to the Task Group.

functions.name: yd_commands.submit.generate_batch_of_tasks_for_task_group
documentation: Generate a batch of tasks for subsequent addition to a task group.

functions.name: yd_commands.submit.submit_batch_of_tasks_to_task_group
documentation:
Submit a batch of tasks to a task group. Return the number of tasks
submitted.

functions.name: yd_commands.submit.get_task_data_property
documentation:
Get the 'taskData' property, either using the contents of the file
specified in 'taskDataFile' or using the string specified in 'taskData'.
Raise exception if both 'taskData' and 'taskDataFile' are set at the same
level in the Work Requirement.

functions.name: yd_commands.submit.follow_progress_old
documentation:
Follow and report the progress of a Work Requirement.
Deprecated temporarily due to problems with Python 3.10+.

functions.name: yd_commands.submit.follow_progress
documentation:
Follow and report the progress of a Work Requirement.
Replacement for the SDK version above.

functions.name: yd_commands.submit.on_update
documentation: Print status messages on Work Requirement update

functions.name: yd_commands.submit.cleanup_on_failure
documentation: Clean up the Work Requirement and any uploaded Objects on failure

functions.name: yd_commands.submit.deduplicate_inputs
documentation:
Deduplicate a list of TaskInputs. This is useful when wildcards
are used. Note that TaskInputs that differ only in their verification
type will be caught by 'check_for_duplicates_in_file_lists()'.

functions.name: yd_commands.submit.check_for_duplicates_in_file_lists
documentation:
Tests for duplicates in file lists. If duplicates found, print an error
and raise an Exception.

functions.name: yd_commands.submit.formatted_number_str
documentation:
Return a nicely formatted number string given a current item number
and a total number of items.

functions.name: yd_commands.submit.get_task_name
documentation:
Create the name of a Task.
Supports lazy substitution.

functions.name: yd_commands.submit.get_task_group_name
documentation:
Create the name of a Task Group.
Supports lazy substitution.

functions.name: yd_commands.submit.create_task
documentation: Create a Task object, handling special processing for specific Task Types.

functions.name: yd_commands.submit.submit_json_raw
documentation:
Submit a 'raw' JSON Work Requirement, consisting of a combined Work
Requirement definition and the constituent Tasks.

Note that there is no automatic upload of required ('VERIFY_AT_START')
input files. These can be pre-uploaded using yd-upload.

functions.name: yd_commands.submit.submit_json_task_batch
documentation: Submit a batch of tasks using the REST API. Return the number of tasks submitted.

modules.name: yd_commands.cloudwizard_common
documentation: Base class and utilities for Cloud Wizard.

classes.name: yd_commands.cloudwizard_common.CommonCloudConfig

methods.name: yd_commands.cloudwizard_common.CommonCloudConfig._remove_yd_templates_by_prefix
documentation: Remove YellowDog Compute Source & Requirement Templates based on a prefix.

methods.name: yd_commands.cloudwizard_common.CommonCloudConfig._generate_static_compute_requirement_template
documentation:
Generate a static compute requirement resource definition
from a list of source names. Strategy can be one of:
- SingleSource
- Split
- Waterfall
Instance type must be a valid AWS instance type.

methods.name: yd_commands.cloudwizard_common.CommonCloudConfig._generate_static_compute_requirement_template_spot_ondemand_waterfall
documentation:
Generate a static Waterfall compute requirement resource definition from
lists of spot and on-demand source names.
Instance type must be a valid AWS instance type.

Re-sequence resources so that possible dependencies are evaluated in the
correct order. If 'creation_or_update' is True this is a creation/update
action, otherwise it's a removal action -- the sequencing differs for each.

modules.name: yd_commands.rich_console_input_fixed
documentation:
This fixes an issue using Rich's Console.input() method, where the prompt
is deleted if a backspace is typed. The problematic method in the base
Console class is overridden.

Bug:
  https://github.com/Textualize/rich/issues/2293

The fix is extracted from the commit that introduces the bug:
  https://github.com/Textualize/rich/commit/568b9517b63282ac781a907d82b0c2965242be54

integer (zero-based).

modules.name: yd_commands.variables
documentation: Utilities for applying variable substitutions.

functions.name: yd_commands.variables.add_substitutions_without_overwriting
documentation:
Add a dictionary of substitutions. Do not overwrite existing values, but
resolve remaining variables if possible.

functions.name: yd_commands.variables.add_or_update_substitution
documentation: Add a substitution to the dictionary, overwriting existing values.

functions.name: yd_commands.variables.process_variable_substitutions_insitu
documentation:
Process a dictionary or list representing JSON or TOML data.
Updates the dictionary in-situ.

Optional 'prefix' and 'postfix' allow variable substitutions intended
for client-side processing to be disambiguated from those to be passed
through for server-side processing.

functions.name: yd_commands.variables.process_variable_substitutions
documentation:
Process type-tagged and non-type-tagged variables, returning the required
type if there's a type-tagged variable at the start of the input string.

functions.name: yd_commands.variables.process_untyped_variable_substitutions
documentation:
Apply untyped variable substitutions to a supplied input string,
including applying default values if present and required.
Uses recursion to process variables from the innermost level outwards.

functions.name: yd_commands.variables.process_typed_variable_substitution
documentation:
Process a single typed substitution, returning the appropriate type.
Assumes there is a substitution present.

functions.name: yd_commands.variables.resolve_filename
documentation:
Check whether 'files_directory' is redundant.
This is a suboptimal approach, but works for now.

functions.name: yd_commands.variables.load_json_file_with_variable_substitutions
documentation:
Takes a JSON filename and returns a dictionary with its variable
substitutions processed.

functions.name: yd_commands.variables.load_jsonnet_file_with_variable_substitutions
documentation:
Takes a Jsonnet filename and returns a dictionary with its variable
substitutions processed.

functions.name: yd_commands.variables.load_toml_file_with_variable_substitutions
documentation:
Takes a TOML filename and returns a dictionary with its variable
substitutions processed.

functions.name: yd_commands.variables.process_variable_substitutions_in_file_contents
documentation: Process substitutions in the raw contents of a complete file.

classes.name: yd_commands.variables.VariableSubstitutedJsonnetFile

methods.name: yd_commands.variables.VariableSubstitutedJsonnetFile.__enter__
documentation:
Return the filename of the temporary variable-processed
jsonnet file.

The jsonnet 'evaluate_file' function will only operate on files,
not strings, so this context manager class will create a
temporary, variable-processed file that can be used by the
evaluator, then deleted.

methods.name: yd_commands.cloudwizard_aws.AWSConfig.set_ssh_ingress_rule
documentation:
Add or remove SSH ingress for all relevant security groups.
A list of regions can be supplied as an argument.
The 'operation' argument must be 'add-ssh' or 'remove-ssh'.
